seoTitle: Sunshine vs Sunshine Bay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Sunshine and Sunshine Bay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Sunshine vs Sunshine Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sunshine and Sunshine Bay.
heading: Sunshine vs Sunshine Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sunshine, New South Wales and Sunshine Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sunshine Bay has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Sunshine (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sunshine or Sunshine Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sunshine has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Sunshine Bay scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sunshine scores 24/100 for bushfire risk. Sunshine Bay scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sunshine or Sunshine Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sunshine Bay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
